title: Questions from Quarantine: Global Restrictions on the U.S.
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article introduces a weekly web series titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' hosted by Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux as part of the Security and Global Health Project. The series aims to address public inquiries related to living through a global crisis, offering advice on maintaining mental and physical well-being during quarantine. It invites viewers to submit questions via social media using the hashtag #QuestionsFromQuarantine. The content is framed within the context of the coronavirus pandemic, highlighting themes of global health, public health security, and crisis management. The piece also references related expert analyses on topics such as mega-event safety, supply chain management, and potential migration crises, positioning the discussion within broader global policy and security considerations.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为“隔离问题”的每周网络系列节目，由朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，隶属于安全与全球健康项目。该系列旨在回应公众在全球危机期间提出的问题，就如何在隔离期间保持身心健康提供建议。文章鼓励观众通过社交媒体使用#QuestionsFromQuarantine标签提交问题。内容以冠状病毒大流行为背景，突出全球健康、公共卫生安全以及危机管理等主题。文章还引用了相关专家分析，涉及大型活动安全规划、供应链管理以及潜在移民危机等议题，将讨论置于更广泛的全球政策与安全框架内。
